tourism_samples.title: Galle Fort
tourism_samples.description: Galle Fort, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is a historic coastal fortress with cobblestone streets, colonial buildings, museums, and cultural events.

tourism_samples.title: Unawatuna Beach
tourism_samples.description: Unawatuna is a famous beach near Galle with calm turquoise waters, snorkeling spots, and beachside cafes.

tourism_samples.title: Hikkaduwa Beach
tourism_samples.description: Hikkaduwa is a lively beach town known for surfing, coral reefs, nightlife, and seafood restaurants.

tourism_samples.title: Mirissa Beach
tourism_samples.description: Mirissa is a southern coastal town with golden beaches, turquoise waters, whale watching, surfing, and local seafood restaurants.

tourism_samples.title: Bentota Beach
tourism_samples.description: Bentota is a popular beach resort town with water sports, mangrove boat rides, and luxury hotels.

tourism_samples.title: Sigiriya Rock Fortress
tourism_samples.description: Sigiriya, known as Lion Rock, is an ancient fortress with frescoes, water gardens, and terraced landscapes. Visitors can climb to the summit for panoramic views.

tourism_samples.title: Dambulla Cave Temple
tourism_samples.description: Dambulla is a UNESCO-listed cave temple complex with ancient Buddha statues and murals carved into rock.

tourism_samples.title: Anuradhapura Sacred City
tourism_samples.description: Anuradhapura is an ancient city with massive stupas, monasteries, and sacred Buddhist heritage sites.

tourism_samples.title: Temple of the Tooth Relic
tourism_samples.description: Located in Kandy, this sacred temple houses the tooth relic of Buddha and hosts daily religious rituals.

tourism_samples.title: Kandy Lake
tourism_samples.description: Kandy Lake is a scenic man-made lake in the heart of Kandy, perfect for evening walks and views of the Temple of the Tooth.

tourism_samples.title: Ella
tourism_samples.description: Ella is a tranquil hill town with lush tea plantations, waterfalls, and scenic hiking trails like Ella Rock and Little Adam’s Peak.

tourism_samples.title: Nine Arches Bridge
tourism_samples.description: The Nine Arches Bridge in Ella is a famous colonial-era railway bridge surrounded by jungle and tea fields.

tourism_samples.title: Horton Plains National Park
tourism_samples.description: Horton Plains is a highland plateau with cloud forests, grasslands, waterfalls, and the dramatic World’s End cliff.

tourism_samples.title: Nuwara Eliya
tourism_samples.description: Nuwara Eliya, known as Little England, is a cool hill town with tea plantations, lakes, waterfalls, and colonial architecture.

tourism_samples.title: Yala National Park
tourism_samples.description: Yala National Park is a wildlife sanctuary famous for leopards, elephants, and safari jeep tours.

tourism_samples.title: Udawalawe National Park
tourism_samples.description: Udawalawe is a popular wildlife park known for elephant sightings and wide open grasslands.

tourism_samples.title: Trincomalee
tourism_samples.description: Trincomalee is a coastal city with pristine beaches, Koneswaram Temple, diving spots, and historic forts.

tourism_samples.title: Nilaveli Beach
tourism_samples.description: Nilaveli is a beautiful white-sand beach near Trincomalee, ideal for snorkeling and relaxation.

tourism_samples.title: Arugam Bay
tourism_samples.description: Arugam Bay is a world-renowned surfing destination on the east coast, known for its laid-back vibe, point breaks, and vibrant nightlife.

tourism_samples.title: Polonnaruwa Ancient City
tourism_samples.description: The second ancient capital of Sri Lanka, featuring well-preserved ruins of royal palaces, temples, and the magnificent Gal Vihara rock sculptures.

tourism_samples.title: Galle Fort Dutch Museum
tourism_samples.description: The Dutch Period Museum inside Galle Fort showcases colonial artifacts, furniture, and history of Dutch rule in Sri Lanka.

tourism_samples.title: Galle Lighthouse
tourism_samples.description: The Galle Lighthouse is the oldest lighthouse in Sri Lanka, located within the Galle Fort walls with panoramic ocean views.

tourism_samples.title: Jungle Beach
tourism_samples.description: Jungle Beach is a secluded cove near Unawatuna surrounded by jungle, ideal for snorkeling and escaping the crowds.

tourism_samples.title: Japanese Peace Pagoda
tourism_samples.description: A serene Buddhist stupa on a hilltop near Unawatuna with stunning views over the Indian Ocean and surrounding coastline.

tourism_samples.title: Koggala Lake
tourism_samples.description: Koggala Lake near Galle is a scenic lagoon perfect for boat rides, birdwatching, and visiting small island temples.

tourism_samples.title: Rumassala Hill
tourism_samples.description: Rumassala is a forested hill near Unawatuna with jungle trails, medicinal herbs, and panoramic views of Galle Bay.

tourism_samples.title: Hikkaduwa Coral Reef
tourism_samples.description: The Hikkaduwa coral reef is a protected marine sanctuary near Galle with vibrant coral gardens and sea turtles.

tourism_samples.title: Kataragama Sacred City
tourism_samples.description: Kataragama is a multi-religious sacred town in southern Sri Lanka, home to the famous Kataragama Devalaya shrine, Buddhist dagobas, and the Menik Ganga river used for ritual bathing. It is revered by Buddhists, Hindus, Muslims, and indigenous Veddas.

tourism_samples.title: Pinnawala Elephant Orphanage
tourism_samples.description: Pinnawala Elephant Orphanage is a world-famous sanctuary that rescues and rehabilitates orphaned wild elephants. Visitors can watch elephants bathing in the Maha Oya river and interact with the largest captive elephant herd in the world.

tourism_samples.title: Ambuluwawa Tower
tourism_samples.description: Ambuluwawa Tower is a unique spiral biodiversity complex and tower located in Gampola, offering panoramic views of four provinces. The site features a multi-religious center, forest trails, and diverse flora and fauna.

tourism_samples.title: Knuckles Mountain Range
tourism_samples.description: The Knuckles Mountain Range is a UNESCO World Heritage listed mountain range in central Sri Lanka, known for its dramatic peaks, cloud forests, waterfalls, diverse wildlife, and challenging trekking trails.

tourism_samples.title: Jaffna Fort
tourism_samples.description: A historic fortress in the northern tip of Sri Lanka, reflecting both Portuguese and Dutch architectural influences and offering views of the Jaffna lagoon.

tourism_samples.title: Pidurangala Rock
tourism_samples.description: Pidurangala is a massive rock formation situated adjacent to Sigiriya Rock, offering an adventurous hike and the best panoramic sunset views of Sigiriya Lion Rock.

tourism_samples.title: Ritigala Archaeological Monastery
tourism_samples.description: Ritigala is an ancient Buddhist monastery and mountain range strict nature reserve containing beautiful stone ruins, bridges, and unique medicinal herbs.

tourism_samples.title: Delft Island
tourism_samples.description: Also known as Neduntheevu, Delft Island is a remote island off the Jaffna coast famous for its wild horses, giant baobab tree, growing coral walls, and historical Dutch ruins.

tourism_samples.title: Lipton's Seat
tourism_samples.description: Lipton's Seat is a famous panoramic viewpoint in Haputale where Sir Thomas Lipton used to survey his vast tea plantations, offering breathtaking views over multiple provinces.

tourism_samples.title: Diyaluma Falls
tourism_samples.description: Diyaluma Falls is the second-highest waterfall in Sri Lanka, popular for its natural swimming pools and infinity drop pools situated at the very top of the falls.

tourism_samples.title: Lunuganga Estate
tourism_samples.description: The country estate of the world-famous tropical modernist architect Geoffrey Bawa, displaying beautiful Italian-style gardens, lake views, and artistic landscaping.

tourism_samples.title: Pigeon Island National Park
tourism_samples.description: A beautiful marine national park in Nilaveli consisting of pristine coral reefs, sea turtles, colorful reef fish, and harmless blacktip reef sharks ideal for snorkeling.

tourism_samples.title: Sinharaja Forest Reserve
tourism_samples.description: Sinharaja is a UNESCO World Heritage biosphere reserve and primary tropical rainforest in Sri Lanka, boasting immense biodiversity and rare endemic birds and tree species.

tourism_samples.title: Bambarakanda Falls
tourism_samples.description: Bambarakanda is the tallest waterfall in Sri Lanka, cascading down a sheer rock face amidst beautiful pine forests.

tourism_samples.title: Adam's Peak
tourism_samples.description: Known locally as Sri Pada, Adam's Peak is a sacred mountain revered by multiple religions, famous for midnight pilgrimage hikes to watch the magnificent sunrise and its triangular shadow.

tourism_samples.title: Gal Oya National Park
tourism_samples.description: A remote wildlife sanctuary famous for its boat safaris on the Senanayake Samudra reservoir, where visitors can watch wild elephants swimming between islands.

tourism_samples.title: Belilena Cave
tourism_samples.description: An archaeological cave site near Kitulgala that holds evidence of the prehistoric Balangoda Man, who lived there over 12,000 years ago.

tourism_samples.title: Mannar Island
tourism_samples.description: A northern peninsula containing ancient baobab trees, historic Mannar Fort, migrating flamingos, and the sandbanks of Adam's Bridge stretching towards India.

tourism_samples.title: Nilgala Forest Reserve
tourism_samples.description: A medicinal forest reserve renowned for its savannah-like grasslands, wild herds of deer, rich medicinal herbs, and avian biodiversity.

tourism_samples.title: Dunhinda Falls
tourism_samples.description: One of Sri Lanka's most beautiful roaring waterfalls, located near Badulla and surrounded by lush green valleys.
